title: User invokes playbook with default trigger phrase, only Phase 1 runs
steps: User says 'run the quality playbook' or 'execute the quality playbook'
Skill starts a clean session and runs Phase 1 (Explore)
Phase 1 produces output files on disk
Skill stops after Phase 1 and reports what happened
Skill provides the user with the exact phrase to say to continue
trigger: User says 'run the quality playbook' or 'execute the quality playbook'
acceptance: Phase 1 runs and the skill stops; the user receives a summary of what happened and the exact prompt to say next; no further phases execute automatically.

title: User explicitly advances through phases one at a time across sessions
steps: User triggers a phase (Phase 1 by default or a subsequent phase explicitly)
Skill runs the requested phase in a clean session with the full context window
Phase writes output files on disk for the next phase to read
Skill stops and reports results along with the continuation prompt
User decides whether to continue and provides an explicit instruction to advance
trigger: User provides an explicit instruction naming or implying a specific phase to run
acceptance: Each phase runs in isolation with its own context window; the skill does not proceed to the next phase without explicit user instruction; disk artifacts from each phase are available for subsequent phases.

title: Phase 0 full continuation mode: prior runs exist with conformant artifacts
steps: Detect that quality/runs/ exists and contains at least one subdirectory with quality/BUGS.md (conformant artifact).
Enter continuation mode.

Step 0a: Glob quality/runs/*/quality/BUGS.md; for each confirmed bug extract bug ID, file:line, summary, and regression test assertion; deduplicate by file:line; write merged seed list to quality/SEED_CHECKS.md in the required table format.

Step 0b: For each seed in SEED_CHECKS.md, run the assertion against the current source tree; record PASS or FAIL; add failing seeds to this run's BUGS.md unconditionally; add passing seeds to PROGRESS.md as 'SEED-NNN: resolved since prior run'.

Step 0c: Read quality/runs/*/quality/PROGRESS.md; extract scope declarations; compile list of previously covered subsystems; if uncovered subsystems exist, flag them for Phase 1 prioritization; if all subsystems covered, flag for different-emphasis re-exploration.
Step 0d: Prepend seed context to the Phase 3 code review prompt and to quality/RUN_SPEC_AUDIT.md using the prescribed language.
Proceed to Phase 1 with seed injection complete and scope guidance recorded.
trigger: The playbook runner starts a new quality run and quality/runs/ is found to exist and contain at least one subdirectory with a quality/BUGS.md file.
acceptance: quality/SEED_CHECKS.md is written with all deduplicated prior-run confirmed bugs; all seed assertions have been executed and recorded; failing seeds appear in BUGS.md; passing seeds appear in PROGRESS.md; Phase 3 prompt and RUN_SPEC_AUDIT.md contain the required seed injection text; Phase 1 scope guidance reflects uncovered subsystems.

title: Phase 0 skip: no prior runs exist
steps: Check whether quality/runs/ exists.
Determine that quality/runs/ does not exist.
Skip Phase 0 entirely.
Proceed directly to Phase 1.
trigger: The playbook runner starts a quality run on a project that has no quality/runs/ directory (first-ever run or runs directory was never created).
acceptance: Phase 0 is not executed; no SEED_CHECKS.md is written; the runner advances directly to Phase 1 without any seed injection or scope analysis.

title: Phase 0 partial skip: quality/runs/ exists but contains no conformant artifacts
steps: Check whether quality/runs/ exists — it does.
Check for conformant artifacts (subdirectories containing quality/BUGS.md) — none are found (directory is empty or subdirectories lack BUGS.md).
Skip Step 0a (no prior BUGS.md to read).
Fall through to Step 0b, which in this case finds no seeds to execute (seed list is empty).
Continue to Phase 1 without seed injection.
trigger: The playbook runner starts a quality run and quality/runs/ exists but is empty or contains only non-conformant subdirectories (no quality/BUGS.md present in any of them).
acceptance: Step 0a is skipped; no SEED_CHECKS.md is written (or it is written empty with zero seeds); Phase 0b executes with an empty seed list and records nothing; the runner proceeds to Phase 1 without any carry-forward bugs injected.

title: User runs Phase 3 code review and regression tests
steps: User explicitly requests Phase 3 (e.g., 'Run quality playbook phase 3' or 'keep going' from Phase 2 end-of-phase message).
Skill re-reads `quality/PROGRESS.md` and `quality/REQUIREMENTS.md` to establish the requirements target list.
Skill reads `references/review_protocols.md` for the three-pass code review protocol and regression test closure mandate.
Skill executes all three passes of the code review protocol against the codebase, using REQUIREMENTS.md as the target list.
Skill identifies confirmed BUGs from the code review findings.
Skill writes a regression test for every confirmed BUG per the closure mandate in review_protocols.md.

Skill updates PROGRESS.md: adds each confirmed BUG to the cumulative BUG tracker with source 'Code Review', file:line reference, description, severity, and closure status (regression test function name or exemption reason).
Skill marks Phase 3 (Code review + regression tests) complete in PROGRESS.md.

Skill prints the mandatory end-of-phase message: heading '# Phase 3 Complete — Code Review', summary of N bugs confirmed / N regression test patches / N fix patches, list of bug IDs with one-line summaries, and instructions to continue to Phase 4.
Skill stops and waits for explicit user instruction before starting Phase 4.
trigger: User explicitly requests Phase 3 execution, either by saying 'Run quality playbook phase 3' or by saying 'keep going' after the Phase 2 end-of-phase message.
acceptance: All three code review passes complete; regression tests written for every confirmed BUG; PROGRESS.md updated with all BUG tracker entries and Phase 3 marked complete; mandatory end-of-phase message printed with correct summary counts and bug list; skill stops without auto-advancing to Phase 4.

title: Full-council Phase 4 spec audit and triage with spot-check validation
steps: Receive trigger to run Phase 4 after Phase 3 completes
Consult references/spec_audit.md for the Council of Three protocol, triage process, and verification probes

For each spot-check claim in the spec audit prompt, extract actual code at the cited lines and report what those lines contain — not whether the claim sounds plausible; treat claims from docs/requirements as hypotheses
Run the full spec audit protocol with all three Council members producing independent auditor reports
Produce a triage synthesis that includes a '## Pre-audit docs validation' section (using the template from references/spec_audit.md); if docs_gathered/ is empty, note the baseline used
Ensure every verification probe in the triage produces executable evidence (test assertions with line-number citations)
Categorize each confirmed finding
Add every confirmed code bug to PROGRESS.md BUG tracker with source 'Spec Audit'
trigger: User says 'Run quality playbook phase 4' or 'keep going' after Phase 3 completion message
acceptance: Triage report exists with '## Pre-audit docs validation' section; every verification probe has executable evidence; all confirmed findings are categorized; all confirmed code bugs appear in PROGRESS.md BUG tracker with source 'Spec Audit'.

title: Phase 4 spec audit with incomplete council and enumeration or whitelist checks
actors: Incomplete Council (fewer than 3/3 auditors returned usable reports)
steps: Receive trigger to run Phase 4
Determine that effective council is fewer than 3/3 (one or more auditors did not return usable reports)
Identify whether the run includes whitelist, enumeration, dispatch-function, or carried-forward seed checks
If such checks are present and council is incomplete: do not conclude 'no confirmed defects' for those checks using prose-only validation
If prose-only validation is all that is available, escalate those checks to 'NEEDS VERIFICATION'
Run the mechanical check (executed proof artifacts) for the affected checks before closing the finding
If mechanical verification artifacts are produced, an incomplete council is acceptable; proceed with triage using the mechanical evidence
Complete remaining triage steps: Pre-audit docs validation section, categorize confirmed findings, update PROGRESS.md BUG tracker with source 'Spec Audit'
trigger: Phase 4 begins but one or more Council members fail to return a usable report, and the run contains enumeration, whitelist, dispatch-function, or carried-forward seed checks
acceptance: Affected checks are either closed with executed mechanical proof artifacts or escalated to 'NEEDS VERIFICATION' — not closed with prose-only validation; the audit does not conclude 'no confirmed defects' for those checks without mechanical proof.

title: Run Phase 5 reconciliation and closure verification after Phase 4 completes
steps: Verify Phase 5 entry gate: confirm spec_audits/ contains triage and auditor files, PROGRESS.md Phase 4 line is [x]
Re-read PROGRESS.md BUG tracker as authoritative finding list

Scan BUG tracker for auto-trigger patterns and run two-round challenge via fresh sub-agents for each triggered bug; record verdicts in quality/challenge/BUG-NNN-challenge.md; apply verdicts (CONFIRMED/DOWNGRADED/REJECTED)
Run Post-Review Reconciliation per references/requirements_pipeline.md; update COMPLETENESS_REPORT.md
Verify every BUG tracker row has a regression test reference or explicit exemption; write missing tests or exemptions
Re-read triage report; sync all confirmed code bugs into BUGS.md; create BUGS.md if absent
Remove or relocate regression tests for any BUGs reclassified by spec audit as design choices or false positives
Deploy verification probes for any finding where code review and spec audit disagree; record resolutions in BUG tracker
For every tdd-results.json entry with verdict 'TDD verified', confirm red.log (first line RED) and green.log (first line GREEN) exist; downgrade or run tests if missing
Check for contradictions between executed evidence and prose artifacts; correct any contradictory prose artifacts
Read SKILL.md version field; verify all artifact version stamps match exactly
If quality/mechanical/ exists: confirm verify.sh present; run verify.sh; capture mechanical-verify.log and mechanical-verify.exit; fail terminal gate if exit non-zero
Enumerate all confirmed bug IDs; verify BUG-NNN.red.log exists for each; verify BUG-NNN.green.log exists for each with a fix patch; verify first line format
Check terminal gate prerequisites: Phase 3 and Phase 4 complete or explicitly skipped
Verify BUGS.md exists with correct ### BUG-NNN headings; create zero-bug summary if no confirmed bugs
Print mandatory BUG tracker count statement to user; write same into PROGRESS.md ## Terminal Gate Verification section
Verify tracker entry count equals M + L; stop and reconcile if counts mismatch
Grep for each regression test function name referenced in tracker; write missing test functions
Verify 'With docs' metadata field accuracy; correct if wrong
Run artifact file-existence gate: verify all standard and conditional required artifacts exist on disk; create any missing artifacts
Validate sidecar JSON required keys in tdd-results.json and integration-results.json; add missing keys
Locate and run quality_gate.py from project root; fix all FAIL results; append output to quality/results/quality-gate.log
Mark Phase 5 complete in PROGRESS.md with BUG tracker closure status for every entry
Print mandatory end-of-phase message and STOP
trigger: User says 'Run quality playbook phase 5' or 'keep going' after Phase 4 completes with triage and auditor artifacts confirmed present
acceptance: All mandatory gates pass (entry gate, challenge gate, sync gate, TDD log gate, contradiction gate, version stamp gate, mechanical receipt gate, artifact existence gate, sidecar JSON validation, quality_gate.py exits 0); PROGRESS.md Phase 5 line is marked [x] with BUG tracker showing closure status for every entry; mandatory end-of-phase message is printed; agent stops without proceeding to Phase 6.

title: Challenge gate rejects or downgrades false-positive bugs before reconciliation
steps: Scan BUG tracker for confirmed bugs matching auto-trigger patterns (security-class finding, design-decision comment at cited location, no spec basis, sibling code paths differ, missing functionality)

For each triggered bug, engage fresh sub-agent for round 1 challenge: assess whether the finding represents a real defect or a documented design choice, intentional gap, or self-documenting placeholder
Engage second fresh sub-agent for round 2 challenge per references/challenge_gate.md protocol
Record final verdict (CONFIRMED, DOWNGRADED, or REJECTED) in quality/challenge/BUG-NNN-challenge.md
For CONFIRMED bugs: proceed normally with no change to tracker
For DOWNGRADED bugs: adjust severity in BUG tracker to reflect challenge outcome
For REJECTED bugs: remove from BUG tracker; relocate to 'Reviewed and dismissed' appendix in BUGS.md with challenge reasoning
Continue to reconciliation with updated BUG tracker
trigger: At least one confirmed bug in the BUG tracker matches a challenge gate auto-trigger pattern during Phase 5 execution
acceptance: Every triggered bug has a quality/challenge/BUG-NNN-challenge.md verdict file; CONFIRMED bugs are unchanged in the tracker; DOWNGRADED bugs have updated severity; REJECTED bugs are absent from the BUG tracker and present with reasoning in the 'Reviewed and dismissed' appendix of BUGS.md; no finding that represents a documented design decision, self-documenting placeholder, or intentional feature gap survives the challenge.

title: Execute Phase 6 incremental verification after reconciliation completes
steps: User explicitly requests Phase 6 (e.g., 'Run quality playbook phase 6' or 'keep going' after Phase 5 end-of-phase message)
Model loads references/verification.md (45 self-check benchmarks) as required reference for this phase
Model identifies the ordered list of independent verification steps

For each step in sequence: read only the file(s) needed for that step; check the single condition assigned to that step; append the result to quality/results/phase6-verification.log; drop the loaded context before proceeding
Model does not load all artifacts at once and does not hold the full checklist in memory across steps
After all steps complete, model reports Phase 6 outcome to user based on accumulated log entries
trigger: User explicitly requests Phase 6 after Phase 5 end-of-phase message is printed and user says 'Run quality playbook phase 6' or 'keep going'.
acceptance: Phase 6 executes as a series of independent incremental steps each writing to quality/results/phase6-verification.log; no step pre-loads artifacts for other steps; all 45 benchmarks in references/verification.md are applied; the log accumulates results incrementally before each step advances.

title: User verifies applied bug fixes using recheck mode
steps: User (or another agent) applies fixes to one or more bugs identified in a previous full playbook run.
User invokes recheck mode by saying 'recheck', 'verify the bug fixes', or 'check which bugs are fixed'.
Skill reads `quality/BUGS.md` and parses every BUG-NNN entry.
For each bug, skill checks the fix against the current source tree (fix patch check, source inspection, regression test execution).
Skill assigns a status verdict to each bug (FIXED, PARTIALLY_FIXED, or still open).
Skill reports which bugs are fixed vs still open, completing in 2-10 minutes.
trigger: User says 'recheck' or 'verify the bug fixes' or 'check which bugs are fixed' after applying fixes from a prior playbook run.
acceptance: Recheck reads existing `quality/BUGS.md`, checks each bug against the current source tree, and reports which bugs are fixed vs. still open — completing in 2-10 minutes without re-running the full six-phase pipeline.

title: User reviews generated artifacts and directs next steps after Phase 6 completes
steps: Phases 1-6 complete and all artifacts are generated and verified.
Skill enters Phase 7 and presents a scannable summary of all generated artifacts (Part 1).
User scans the summary to understand what was produced.
User requests drill-down on specific artifacts or findings of interest (Part 2, on demand).
Skill responds to drill-down requests with relevant detail.
Skill presents a menu of improvement paths available to the user (Part 3).
User selects one or more improvement paths to pursue next.
trigger: Completion of Phases 1-6 — all artifacts generated and verified.
acceptance: User understands what was generated, has explored what matters to them, and has chosen how to improve it; no artifacts are presented without explanation; all three parts (scannable summary, drill-down on demand, menu of improvement paths) are executed.

title: Agent executes the full four-strategy iteration cycle in recommended order
steps: Complete a baseline run (structured three-stage exploration).
Run the 'gap' strategy: scan previous coverage and explore uncovered subsystems to find bugs missed by the baseline.
After gap completes, agent prints a suggested prompt for the 'unfiltered' strategy.
Run the 'unfiltered' strategy: pure domain-driven exploration with no structure, finding bugs that structured approaches suppress.
After unfiltered completes, agent prints a suggested prompt for the 'parity' strategy.
Run the 'parity' strategy: cross-path comparison and diffing to find inconsistencies between parallel implementations.
After parity completes, agent prints a suggested prompt for the 'adversarial' strategy.
Run the 'adversarial' strategy: challenge dismissed or demoted findings to recover Type II errors from previous triage.
After each strategy, assess cumulative yield; stop cycling when diminishing returns are observed or a stop decision is made.
trigger: User or agent decides to move beyond the baseline run and begin iterative augmentation of coverage
acceptance: All four strategies execute in the order gap → unfiltered → parity → adversarial; a suggested next-strategy prompt is printed after each iteration; the cycle stops when diminishing returns are reached or the user decides to stop

title: Phase C: iterative contract coverage verification and gap remediation loop
steps: Read quality/CONTRACTS.md to obtain the full list of contracts.
Read quality/REQUIREMENTS.md to obtain all requirements and their conditions of satisfaction.

For each contract, determine coverage status: 'covered' (explicitly tested in conditions of satisfaction), 'partially covered' (related requirement exists but misses the specific contract), or 'uncovered' (no requirement addresses the behavior).
Write quality/COVERAGE_MATRIX.md using the prescribed format: header with totals and percentages, then three sections listing fully covered, partially covered, and uncovered contracts.
Fix gaps in quality/REQUIREMENTS.md: add missing conditions of satisfaction to existing requirements or create new requirements for uncovered contracts. Report the changes made.
If uncovered count is now 0, exit the loop and proceed to Phase D.
Otherwise, if fewer than 3 iterations have been completed, return to step 1 with updated files and repeat.
After the third iteration, proceed to Phase D regardless of remaining uncovered count.
trigger: Phase B has produced quality/REQUIREMENTS.md and quality/CONTRACTS.md exists; Phase C is the next step in the pipeline.
acceptance: Loop terminates either when uncovered count reaches 0 (success) or after 3 iterations (max-iteration exit). quality/COVERAGE_MATRIX.md exists with the correct format. quality/REQUIREMENTS.md reflects all gap-fixing changes. Changes are reported.

title: Benchmark operator runs playbook against multiple repos and verifies self-check benchmarks
steps: Operator selects a benchmark repo and runs the playbook end-to-end
Phase 0 self-check benchmarks verify PROGRESS.md convergence sections, BUGS.md presence on completed runs, and mechanical-verify.log on Phase 6
Operator inspects the self-check benchmark log to confirm the run is conformant
Operator compares results against prior benchmark runs to detect regressions
trigger: Operator runs the playbook on a repo from the benchmark set
acceptance: Self-Check Benchmarks pass for the run: PROGRESS.md convergence section is present (continuation mode), BUGS.md exists with confirmed bugs or a positive zero-bugs assertion, and mechanical-verify.log was produced by `bash quality/mechanical/verify.sh` (not a Python substitute).

title: Bootstrap self-audit: skill audited against its own SKILL.md
steps: Maintainer runs the playbook with the QPB repo itself as the target
Phase 0 detects prior self-audit runs in `quality/runs/` if present, otherwise starts fresh
The four-pass derivation (Pass A naive coverage, Pass B mechanical citation, Pass C formal REQs, Pass D coverage audit) runs over QPB's own SKILL.md plus references/*.md
Pass D produces `pass_d_council_inbox.json` listing rejected drafts, tier-5 demotions, and zero-REQ sections that need Council review
Maintainer reviews the council inbox to triage QPB's own coverage gaps
trigger: Maintainer invokes the playbook with `~/Documents/QPB` as the target_dir
acceptance: All four passes reach `status: "complete"`; `quality/phase3/pass_c_formal.jsonl` and `pass_c_formal_use_cases.jsonl` exist with at least one record each; `pass_d_council_inbox.json` schema is valid; the run does not exit non-zero unless an upstream pass refused via the B4 gate.
